mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of friend functions in C++?

options.text: To allow external functions access to private class members
To create inherited relationships between classes
To improve performance of class methods

mcqs.explanation: Friend functions are specifically designed to grant external functions privileged access to private and protected members of a class while maintaining controlled encapsulation.

mcqs.question: Where must a friend function be declared?

options.text: Outside the class using friend keyword
Inside the class using friend keyword

mcqs.explanation: Friend functions must be declared inside the class using the friend keyword, but they are defined outside the class like regular functions.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is TRUE about friend classes?

options.text: Friendship is inherited by derived classes
Friend classes can access all members of the granting class
Friend classes must be defined before the granting class

mcqs.explanation: When a class declares another class as friend, all member functions of the friend class can access private and protected members of the granting class.
The code will output 5 because the friend function showData() has access to the private member 'data' of the Test class.

mcqs.question: Which access specifier does the friend declaration belong to?

options.text: It can be placed under any access specifier

mcqs.explanation: The friend declaration is not affected by access specifiers and can be placed in public, private, or protected sections of the class.
